meaning_changes.explanation: Threshold loosened / rule removed — more sessions become eligible.
meaning_changes.grounding: Loosening a numeric gate or removing a rule widens the eligible audience.

constraint_results.grounding: Rokt publishes "sub-200ms latency"; <rokt-thank-you fallback-timeout> defaults to 5000ms.
Rokt: "If no offer is eligible, the wrapped content renders normally." Fallback must be No Offer Rendered.

constraint_results.detail: No sensitive attribute used without consent.
constraint_results.grounding: Rokt exposes noFunctional/noTargeting consent flags; sensitive PII must be consent-gated.
Rokt Ads policies prohibit categories (gambling/alcohol/tobacco) without age gating.
Frequency management: raising impression caps increases exposure/fatigue risk.
Rokt One Platform Page Holdout: a recommended 5% control before rollout.
Rokt: "Material changes to a campaign's privacy policy, disclaimers, or terms and conditions trigger the approval process."
Rokt Manage-a-campaign: objective/country/language/timezone cannot be edited — "you need to create a new campaign".

constraint_results.detail: age gate 2 is out of plausible range (13-100) — likely a data-entry error.
constraint_results.grounding: Operational guard (not a Rokt-doc rule): catch data-entry errors before subtler checks — inspired by ShelfTrace's execution-gate plausibility guard.
Rokt Audience targeting: "Include (is not in)" vs "Exclude (is in)" differ only on MISSING values.

ope_prescreen.note: Support-guard only, not a lift estimate. Real off-policy evaluation needs logged action propensities; the online holdout remains mandatory.

reasons: plausibility FAIL: age gate 2 is out of plausible range (13-100) — likely a data-entry error.
